evidence_basis_summary: The preprint and APE page frame the task as testing pre-specified hypotheses on a fixed NYSE TAQ dataset (P1) and running automated tournaments on public data (S1); neither case turns on human selection of which materials belong in the analysis universe.
notes: Upgraded from low to high: full-text confirms no relevance-adjudication pressure in this case.

evidence_basis_summary: C024_P1 documents that 150 autonomous agents diverge on measure-choice forks (autocorrelation vs variance ratio, dollar vs share volume) and that AI agents omit relative-change specifications entirely, making human constraint on acceptable measure/mechanism logic the central finding of the case.
notes: Upgraded from low to high: case is a canonical illustration of mechanism-disciplinarian pressure (codebook lists case 24 as an example); preprint directly discusses measure-choice forks.

evidence_basis_summary: The six hypotheses about market quality trends are taken as given from Menkveld et al. (2024) rather than renegotiated; C024_P1 describes no construct-definition or pluralism boundary work.
notes: Upgraded from low to high: full-text shows constructs are inherited not contested.

evidence_basis_summary: Agents share a single fixed corpus (NYSE TAQ SPY 2015-2024, 66 GB) in C024_P1; APE similarly uses shared public data (C024_S1). No role for keeping multiple corpora alive.
notes: Upgraded from low to high: single-corpus design explicit in both sources.

evidence_basis_summary: C024_P1 explicitly designs a three-stage feedback protocol (independent analysis, AI peer review, exemplar exposure) mirroring Menkveld et al.; APE publishes methodology, code, data, and failures openly and runs a structured TrueSkill tournament with position-swapping controls.
notes: Upgraded from low to high: protocol/provenance design is front-and-center in both sources.

evidence_basis_summary: C024_P1 explicitly warns that convergence reflects imitation not understanding and that AI multiverses may be structurally narrower than human ones, raising transportability of AI-generated estimates; C024_S1 warns papers should not be used for evidence-based policy making without peer review.
notes: Upgraded from low to high: both sources foreground transportability/credibility concerns.

evidence_basis_summary: The pipeline is fully autonomous on archival market microstructure data with no human intervention (C024_P1); no field, interview, or interpretive setting exists.
notes: Upgraded from low to high: explicit no-human-in-loop design.

evidence_basis_summary: C024_S1 carries explicit disclaimers that APE papers are not peer-reviewed, may contain hallucinations/fabricated results, and should not inform policy; C024_P1 frames policy implications of AI-generated empirics, both pushing accountability back onto human researchers and institutions.
notes: Upgraded from low to high: accountability caveats are prominent in both sources.

evidence_basis_summary: Neither source shows humans negotiating category boundaries or codebook rules; hypotheses and measures are drawn from Menkveld et al. or left to agents.
notes: Upgraded from low to high: no construct-definition work in the case.

evidence_basis_summary: C024_P1 studies dispersion across agents but does not treat subgroup perspectives or representational pluralism; divergence is measured as NSE, not preserved as legitimate plural views.
notes: Upgraded from low to high: dispersion framed as noise to characterize not plurality to preserve.

evidence_basis_summary: C024_P1 explicitly stresses that exemplar-driven convergence reflects imitation not understanding and cautions about automated policy evaluation; C024_S1 warns AI papers should not inform policy, directly pressing the travel-across-settings question.
notes: Upgraded from low to high: case is a codebook example for this split (case 24 listed under mechanism disciplinarian but transportability pressure is directly textually supported).

evidence_basis_summary: C024_P1's Stage 3 uses five highest-rated exemplar papers as calibration anchors that collapse IQR 80-99%, and C024_S1 runs a TrueSkill tournament benchmarking APE papers against AER/AEJ human papers with a Gemini-3.1 judge; both are explicit benchmarking/calibration designs.
notes: Upgraded from low to high: calibration design is central to both sources.

evidence_basis_summary: Fully autonomous pipeline on archival TAQ data (C024_P1); no interviewer or fieldworker role exists.
No tacit/archival interpretive work is described; agents autonomously explore structured market data (C024_P1).
No triage or abstract-screening task exists; hypotheses are fixed and agents operate end-to-end on raw data (C024_P1).

role_label: Routine coder on high-consensus, ground-truth-rich tasks
evidence_basis_summary: No labeling task with external ground truth is present; the case is about methodological choice dispersion not coder-auditor shift (C024_P1).

Agents write 2,000-4,000-word research reports autonomously (C024_P1) and APE produces full academic papers end-to-end (C024_S1), indicating AI drafting occurs, but neither source frames prose polishing as a distinct shrinking human task cluster within the case.
notes: Upgraded from low to medium: AI drafting is observed but the case's focus is on empirical-analysis variation, not on displacing a human polisher role; kept observable=no since the role is not separately exercised by humans here.
